explanation: The requirement states that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator should not command transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec. The atomic proposition 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' represents when such a prohibited change occurs. The correct LTL expression must ensure that this proposition is never true at any point in time, which is best captured by the invariant 'G !transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50'. This prohibits the system from commanding these roll accelerations globally (at all times).

The requirement 'The Inner Loop Roll Regulator Shall not command transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec.' means that at any time, it should never happen that the system commands a transient angular roll acceleration change greater than the specified limit. The proposition 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' directly models this forbidden condition. Using the LTL formula G(!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50), we express that globally (at every point in time along a run), it is always not the case that a transient change above the threshold occurs, which directly and strictly captures the intent of the natural language statement. No numerical comparison operators are used, relying only on the provided atomic proposition. Any alternative approach, such as only eventually forbidding the property (F(!...)), would not be strict enough, as the requirement is global and absolute: it must never be violated.

The requirement specifies that transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec must not be commanded by the Inner Loop Roll Regulator at any time. In terms of LTL, this means that it must always be the case that 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' is not true. The formula G(!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50) globally prohibits such transient acceleration changes. This directly encodes the requirement; numerical comparison is encapsulated in the atomic proposition. No previously given LTL is present, so no alternative explanation is needed.

The requirement states that the system (the Inner Loop Roll Regulator) must not ever command 'transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec.' The appropriate atomic proposition is 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50', which represents the forbidden event. This is a safety property, best expressed by the 'Globally' operator G: globally, it should never happen. So the LTL formula is: globally not (transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50). This captures the requirement that such transient changes are not permitted at any time by the regulator.

The requirement specifies that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator should never command (i.e., cause or allow) transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50 deg/sec^2/sec. This corresponds to asserting that 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' should never be true at any time. The LTL property 'G !transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' ensures that at all times (globally), such transient changes do not occur. This captures the intent of the requirement as a hard safety constraint. Properties using only 'F' (eventually) or omitting the 'G' (globally) operator would not enforce this prohibition at all times, and allowing even one such occurrence would violate the system's safety intent.

The requirement states that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator must never command transient changes in angular roll acceleration that exceed 50deg/sec2/sec. In LTL, this corresponds to specifying that the atomic proposition 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' should never hold (i.e., is globally false). Therefore, we use the globally operator G and apply it to the negation of that atomic proposition: G(!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50). This expresses that at no time may a transient angular roll acceleration change greater than 50deg/sec2/sec be commanded. The alternative proposition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' is not the most accurate for this very specific and transient-based rule, as 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' directly captures the natural language requirement.

The requirement states that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator must not command any transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec. The relevant atomic proposition provided is 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50', which directly expresses when such a transient change occurs. To capture the prohibition on these changes at any time, we use the LTL formula G(!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50), which means 'globally, it is always not the case that a transient change in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec occurs'. This directly enforces the requirement and prohibits the undesired event from ever happening. No other LTL formula is needed or more appropriate given the available atomic propositions.

The key requirement in the natural language is that the system should never command transient changes in angular roll acceleration that are greater than 50deg/sec^2/sec. The atomic proposition 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' numerically encodes whether such a transient change is taking place. To say that such changes should NEVER be commanded, we use the LTL 'globally' (G) operator to assert that at all times, 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' is NOT true. Therefore, G (!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50) precisely captures this requirement. Previous incorrect translations might have failed to use the 'globally' operator, or may not have enforced the negation at all time instants, making them too weak.

The natural language states that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator should not command transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50 deg/sec^2/sec. This is most directly encoded by forbidding (globally) the atomic proposition representing 'transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec.' Other formulations (e.g., referencing angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability) would not directly capture 'transient change' as requested by the requirement.

The requirement is that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator must never command transient changes in angular roll acceleration greater than 50deg/sec2/sec. According to the atomic propositions provided, 'transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50' denotes this forbidden event. The correct LTL property is 'G(!transient_angular_roll_acceleration_change_greater_than_50)', which asserts that globally (at all times), such changes do not occur. Any other LTL formula would likely be too permissive (allowing such changes sometimes) or too restrictive (forbidding more than intended).
